differentials.name: loss of sensation to pain + temperature ← interruption of spinothalamic tracts
differentials.raw: loss of sensation to pain + temperature ← interruption of spinothalamic tracts

differentials.name: trophic changes =
differentials.raw: trophic changes = (skin lesions; Charcot joints in 25% affecting shoulder, elbow, wrist)

differentials.name: muscle weakness ← anterior horn cell involvement
differentials.raw: muscle weakness ← anterior horn cell involvement

differentials.name: spasticity, hyperreflexia ← upper motor neuron involvement
differentials.raw: spasticity, hyperreflexia ← upper motor neuron involvement

differentials.name: abnormal plantar reflexes ← pyramidal tract involvement Location:   predominantly lower end of cervical cord; extension into brainstem
differentials.raw: abnormal plantar reflexes ← pyramidal tract involvement Location:   predominantly lower end of cervical cord; extension into brainstem (= syringobulbia)

differentials.name: Tethered cord
differentials.raw: Tethered cord (in up to 25%) DDx:  transient dilatation of the central canal (transient finding in newborns during the first weeks in life)

differentials.name: Postinflammatory syringomyelia subarachnoid hemorrhage, arachnoid adhesions, S/P surgery, infection
differentials.raw: Postinflammatory syringomyelia subarachnoid hemorrhage, arachnoid adhesions, S/P surgery, infection (tuberculosis, syphilis)

differentials.name: Tumor-associated syringomyelia = peritumoral cyst of spinal cord tumor / herniated disk ← circulatory disturbance + thoracic spinal cord atrophy
differentials.raw: Tumor-associated syringomyelia = peritumoral cyst of spinal cord tumor / herniated disk ← circulatory disturbance + thoracic spinal cord atrophy

context: =  SYRINGOMYELIA = SYRINX (used in a general manner reflecting difficulty in classification) =  longitudinally oriented CSF-filled cavities + gliosis within spinal cord frequently involving both parenchyma + central canal Age:   primarily childhood / early adult life Cause:   Chiari I malformation (41%), trauma (28%), neoplasm (15%), idiopathic (15%) CT: √  distinct area of decreased attenuation in spinal cord (100%) √  swollen / normal-sized / atrophic cord √  NO contrast enhancement √  change in shape + size of cord with change in position (rare) √  flattened vertebral border (rare) with inc
